Principles.Title: Nice Enough
Principles.Description: To the common folk (Raccoons, Deer), the Barons are heroes. They do not rob the poor because 'there is no profit in a carcass' and the poor provide the safe houses and intelligence needed to evade the Banks.

Principles.Title: Brutal When Crossed
Principles.Description: If a village informs on a Baron to the Gilded Compass, the 'Robin Hood' mask falls. The Chapter enacts 'Collective Debt,' stripping the settlement of all resources to compensate for the operational risk.

Principles.Title: Punching Down
Principles.Description: A Baron who robs a peasant is considered a 'gutter-runner'—too weak to hit a real target. This is a firing offense (literally).

Consequences.Title: The Unforgivable Sin
Consequences.Description: Robbing a peasant is considered a 'gutter-runner' too weak to hit a real target.

Consequences.Title: Collective Debt
Consequences.Description: The Chapter enacts 'Collective Debt,' stripping the settlement of all resources to compensate for the operational risk.

Leadership.Title: Quartermaster
Leadership.Description: Fences stolen goods and procurer of Dragonstone (D-Dust).

Leadership.Title: Sergeant of Secrets
Leadership.Description: Spymaster and expert in 'Technical Truths' to counter Avian interrogation.

Method.Title: Earning the Patch
Method.Description: This is a physical, bio-mechanical commitment.

Ritual.Title: The Brand
Ritual.Description: The prospect is branded using a nickel/copper emblem attached to the Unstable Dragonstone Core of the station.

Appearance.Title: The Look
Appearance.Description: Members wear double-breasted heavy canvas trench coats dyed to match their brand color.

Monopoly.Market: The Prohibition Kings of the Shatterlands

Client.Title: The Hypocrisy Client
Client.Description: The biggest customers are the Highborn Horse Nobles. The very Senators passing anti-smuggling laws are buying Baron booze for their private salons.

Method.Title: The River Syndicate (Otters)
Method.Description: They use the River Syndicate (Otters) and 'Ghost Barges' to clean the Aetherium Coin earned from illicit sales.

Method.Title: Fight or Die
Method.Description: Unlike pirates who surrender for a plea deal, Barons provide no such choice. They outrun the enemy or engage in suicidal defense. This makes them a 'high-cost' target for security forces.

Equipment.Title: The Fleet
Equipment.Description: Mass-produced Standard Aether-Skiffs, 'chopped and dropped' for speed. They strip heavy armor to outrun Corsairs.

Origins.Description: They were originally honest cargo haulers crushed by Avian taxes and Vaneer audits. They unionized, armed their ships, and realized that smuggling paid better than hauling grain.

Philosophy & Identity.Description: They reject 'Dry-Land Law' (Avian/Vaneer/Scute). They believe the sky cannot be owned by those who only seek to tax it.
